changes.old: One reason why we use your information across [devices\[40\]](https://mbasic.facebook.com/privacy/policy/printable/?entry point=POLICY ANNOTATION%3A2.ex.1-WhyWeUseInformation#annotation-40) is to help us give you a more personalised experience. For example, we might show you an ad on your phone, and later you might use your laptop to click on the ad and buy the product. By combining that information across your devices, we can understand what ads are relevant to you and help businesses measure how well their ad performed.
changes.new: We share information about you with [marketing vendors\[58\]](https://mbasic.facebook.com/privacy/policy/printable/?entry point=POLICY SUBPAGE%3A4.subpage.11-MarketingVendors#annotation-58). For example, we share your device identifier or other identifiers with marketing vendors to help us serve you ads most relevant to your interests.
changes.explanation: The updated policy indicates that your data may now be shared with additional third parties, such as partners, advertisers, service providers, or group companies.
changes.suggested_action: Check which third parties are involved and whether you can opt out of certain types of sharing or limit data transfers in your account settings.

changes.old: For these purposes, we might share your name, email address, who you're friends with and other account information within the Meta Companies.

changes.new: You can also choose to connect third-party apps and services to your Meta account to enhance your experience with Meta Products, including our features that are part of AI at Meta. When you connect to these apps and services, you are requesting us to exchange information with them. We can use this information about you and others in the same ways that we use your information as described in this Policy, and share information to perform actions for you involving those third-party apps and services.

changes.old: If you add accounts to the same Accounts Centre, we also use your information to offer connected experiences and improve your experiences across accounts. This helps us match your activities with your account, if you have one.

changes.old: We also use manual review to analyse content to train our algorithms to review content the same way a person would. This improves our automatic processing, which in turn helps us improve our products.
changes.new: The provision of the Meta Products includes collecting, storing and, where relevant, sharing, profiling, reviewing and curating, and in some instances not only automated processing, but also manual (human) reviewing, to:
changes.explanation: The updated policy indicates expanded tracking or behavioural analytics (e.g., interactions, pages visited, search terms, click activity, or location) which may be used for personalisation or profiling.
changes.suggested_action: Review privacy settings to limit tracking/analytics. Consider disabling personalised ads, restricting cookies, or using privacy tools if concerned about behavioural profiling.

changes.new: Information about the network that you connect your device to, including your IP address

changes.suggested_action: Review privacy settings to limit tracking/analytics. Consider disabling personalised ads, restricting cookies, or using privacy tools if concerned about behavioural profiling.

changes.old: For example, we collect information when people use our Products, even if they don't have an account, to measure how fast our pages load in different countries and whether they're working properly. This helps us identify and fix issues with local networks.
changes.new: To provide measurement and analytics services, we use the information that we collect about you across your accounts on our Products.
changes.explanation: The updated policy introduces or expands the purposes for which your data is used (e.g., advertising, analytics, research, or security) or clarifies the legal basis for processing.
changes.suggested_action: Check whether you are comfortable with these purposes and, where applicable, adjust your consent or opt-out preferences.

changes.old: The bike shop can then see reports showing aggregated statistics about the audience seeing their ads and how their ads are performing. The reports would show statistics to the advertiser that, for example, most of the people who saw or clicked on their ad:
changes.new: For example, an advertiser might ask a measurement vendor to help work out the impact that its ads on Facebook have had on sales. The measurement vendor compares information from us about clicks on the advertiser's Facebook ads with information from the advertiser about product purchases. Then the measurement vendor creates and provides aggregated reports that show the advertiser how its ads are performing.

changes.explanation: The updated policy describes new or enhanced security measures to protect your data, such as encryption, access controls, or monitoring.
changes.suggested_action: This may improve protection of your data. You can still review details to understand what changed.

changes.old: We collect information when you or others use, or exchange information with, AI at Meta, such as:
changes.new: Interactions with our features that are part of AI at Meta and related metadata. For example, information such as content and messages that you or others exchange with these features.
changes.explanation: The policy indicates that additional types of personal or usage data are now collected, including: metadata.
changes.suggested_action: Review whether you are comfortable with these new types of data being collected. If not, adjust your privacy settings or limit the information you provide.

changes.old: We advertise our own products and services on non-Meta ad platforms. We use marketing vendors to deliver these ads. If we use your activity on our Products to tailor these ads, you can opt out of this type of use.
changes.new: When you use our Products, you see ads and sponsored or commercial content, such as product listings in Shops. Meta personalises your ads on and across Meta Company Products. You also see ads shown through Meta Audience Network or commercial content when you visit other apps. We use your information to show you ads paid for by advertisers, businesses and organisations. We want everything that you see to be interesting and useful to you.
changes.explanation: This section of the policy text has been modified.
changes.suggested_action: Read this part of the policy carefully to understand how it affects your data.

changes.new: Our systems automatically process information that we've collected and stored associated with you and others to assess and understand your interests and preferences and provide you with personalised experiences on and across the Meta Company Products in accordance with our terms. changes.old: Identifiers we collect include device IDs, mobile advertiser ID or IDs from games, apps or accounts you use. We also collect Family device IDs or other identifiers unique to Meta Company Products associated with the same device or account.
changes.new: Identifiers that tell your device apart from other users

changes.old: For example, if you give us permission to access your device's camera roll, we collect metadata. This metadata is from and about your photos and videos, and includes the date and time they were made. We use this to do things such as remind you when you have new photos to upload.
